page_title: "Cancom Marketplace Provider"
description: |-
  The Cancom Marketplace Provider is a Terraform provider for the Cancom Marketplace.

# cancom-marketplace Provider

The provider authenticates against the CANCOM Marketplace API and resolves the configured Marketplace user during provider initialization. Subscription resources no longer take a `user_uuid`; they use the user found by `marketplace_user_email`.

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `api_client_id` (String) The API client ID for the Cancom Marketplace - received by OneTime Link. Can also be set with `CANCOM_MARKETPLACE_API_CLIENT_ID`.
- `api_client_secret` (String) The API client secret for the Cancom Marketplace - received by OneTime Link. Can also be set with `CANCOM_MARKETPLACE_API_CLIENT_SECRET`.
- `marketplace_user_email` (String) The marketplace user email address for which subscriptions are created. Can also be set with `CANCOM_MARKETPLACE_USER_EMAIL`.

### Optional

- `api_scope` (String) The API scope for the Cancom Marketplace. Can also be set with `CANCOM_MARKETPLACE_API_SCOPE`. Default is `AT-PROD`.
- `endpoint` (String) The API endpoint for the Cancom Marketplace. Can also be set with `CANCOM_MARKETPLACE_ENDPOINT`. Default is `https://marketplace-apigateway.cancom.de`.
- `azure_client_id` (String) The Azure client ID for the customers tenant. Can also be set with `CANCOM_MARKETPLACE_AZURE_CLIENT_ID`.
- `azure_client_secret` (String) The Azure client secret for the customers tenant. Can also be set with `CANCOM_MARKETPLACE_AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET`.
- `azure_tenant_id` (String) The Azure tenant ID for the customers tenant. Can also be set with `CANCOM_MARKETPLACE_AZURE_TENANT_ID`.

## Environment Variables

Provider arguments can be set in Terraform configuration or through environment variables:

| Provider argument | Environment variable |
| --- | --- |
| `api_client_id` | `CANCOM_MARKETPLACE_API_CLIENT_ID` |
| `api_client_secret` | `CANCOM_MARKETPLACE_API_CLIENT_SECRET` |
| `marketplace_user_email` | `CANCOM_MARKETPLACE_USER_EMAIL` |
| `api_scope` | `CANCOM_MARKETPLACE_API_SCOPE` |
| `endpoint` | `CANCOM_MARKETPLACE_ENDPOINT` |
| `azure_client_id` | `CANCOM_MARKETPLACE_AZURE_CLIENT_ID` |
| `azure_client_secret` | `CANCOM_MARKETPLACE_AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET` |
| `azure_tenant_id` | `CANCOM_MARKETPLACE_AZURE_TENANT_ID` |

If an argument is set both in Terraform configuration and as an environment variable, the Terraform configuration value is used.
If one Azure client-secret argument is set, all three Azure client-secret arguments must be set.

## Marketplace User Resolution

`marketplace_user_email` is resolved through `GET /v1/users` during provider initialization. The provider searches `content[].email` **case-insensitively**.

If no matching user is found, provider initialization fails with:

```text
User not found in CANCOM Marketplace. Contact your Enterprise Administrator
```

If more than one user has the same email address, provider initialization fails with:

```text
User is ambigous in CANCOM Marketplace. Contact your Enterprise Administrator
```

## Azure Authentication

Azure credentials are optional until an operation needs Azure directly. You can either run `az login` in the Terraform environment or set all three provider arguments:

- `azure_client_id`
- `azure_client_secret`
- `azure_tenant_id`

Azure-backed subscription properties currently include `display_name` and `azure_owner_object_id`. Destroy also calls Azure directly to cancel the subscription. Before creating or changing a subscription that needs Azure-backed work, the provider validates Azure authentication and reads the Default Management Group hierarchy settings. This is done before the Marketplace subscription is created to avoid tainted or orphaned state.

See [Troubleshooting](guides/troubleshooting.md) for common errors and required permissions.
